subject: DIAGNOSTIC: unratified + observatory missing from dashboard — root cause + fix

body.summary: All agents (including psychology-agent itself) show 'unreachable' on the interagent dashboard. Root cause: two-layer problem. (1) Running meshd binary (/Users/kashif/Projects/meshd/meshd) does not include CollectRemoteStates — builds a hand-crafted status map without remote_states. (2) The pulse aggregator (ops meshd port 8083) derives StatusURL from agent card discovery URLs pointing to public .safety-quotient.dev domains, but no service serves /api/status at those public URLs.
body.investigation_method: Traced the data path from git show (works) → mesh-status.py CollectRemoteStates (works) → running meshd binary (different codebase) → pulse aggregator (cannot reach public URLs). Verified with curl, ps aux, and direct API queries.

root_causes.title: Running meshd binary lacks CollectRemoteStates
root_causes.detail: The deployed meshd (/Users/kashif/Projects/meshd/meshd, 15MB, Mar 16) builds /api/status via server.go:buildStatusPayload() — a hand-crafted map at lines 468-489 that returns agent_id, version, uptime, health, autonomy_budget, recent_messages, unprocessed_messages, active_gates, deliberation_count, gc_metrics. It does NOT include remote_states, registry_agents, session_summaries, semiotics, or other fields present in the psychology-agent platform/internal/collector/status.go Collect() function.
root_causes.evidence: curl http://localhost:8082/api/status returns keys: [active_gates, agent_id, autonomy_budget, collected_at, db_available, deliberation_count, event_count, gc_metrics, health, recent_deliberations, recent_messages, unprocessed_messages, uptime, uptime_seconds, version]. No remote_states field.

root_causes.title: Pulse aggregator cannot reach agent /api/status endpoints
root_causes.detail: The ops meshd (port 8083) pulse handler discovers agents from their agent cards and derives StatusURL by replacing /.well-known/agent-card.json with /api/status. Agent cards point to public domains (e.g., https://psychology-agent.safety-quotient.dev). No Cloudflare Worker or tunnel serves /api/status at these public URLs. Result: all agents show 'unreachable' in the pulse, including psychology-agent itself despite its meshd running locally on port 8082.
root_causes.evidence: curl -s http://localhost:8083/api/pulse shows all agents with status='unreachable'. curl to public URLs returns empty responses. ps aux shows meshd on ports 8082 (psychology-agent) and 8083 (operations-agent) — both local, neither publicly routed.

data_that_DOES_exist.git_show_works: git show unratified/main:transport/sessions/local-coordination/mesh-state-unratified-agent.json returns valid JSON (budget, actions, transport state). git show observatory/main:... also returns valid JSON. Both remotes fetched within 48 hours.
data_that_DOES_exist.python_mesh_status_works: mesh-status.py _collect_remote_states() correctly reads agent-registry.json, calls git show for each cross-repo-fetch agent, and returns parsed JSON. This code path never executes in production because the deployed binary uses a different status handler.
data_that_DOES_exist.agent_registry_correct: transport/agent-registry.json lists unratified-agent (remote=unratified, transport=cross-repo-fetch) and observatory-agent (remote=observatory, transport=cross-repo-fetch). Git remotes configured and reachable.

fix_options.title: Add CollectRemoteStates to meshd repo
fix_options.description: Port the git-show-based remote state collection from psychology-agent/platform/internal/collector/remote.go into the meshd repo. Add remote_states to buildStatusPayload(). This fixes RC-1 — each agent's meshd instance would include peer state in its own /api/status.
fix_options.complexity: LOW — the Go code already exists in platform/internal/collector/remote.go. Copy and adapt to the meshd repo's registry structure.
fix_options.drawback: Each meshd instance independently runs git show commands — duplicated work across agents.

fix_options.title: Route local meshd ports through Cloudflare Tunnel or Workers
fix_options.description: Set up Cloudflare Tunnel (cloudflared) to route psychology-agent.safety-quotient.dev/api/status → localhost:8082 and operations-agent.safety-quotient.dev/api/status → localhost:8083. This fixes RC-2 — the pulse aggregator could reach all agents' /api/status via their public URLs.
fix_options.complexity: MODERATE — requires cloudflared setup, DNS records, and persistent tunnel process.
fix_options.drawback: Requires Chromabook (the host) to maintain the tunnel. Agent status becomes unavailable when the host goes offline.

fix_options.title: Pulse aggregator uses localhost URLs instead of public URLs
fix_options.description: When meshd instances run on the same host, the pulse aggregator should use localhost:PORT/api/status instead of deriving URLs from agent cards. Add a local_status_url field to the agent registry or meshd config.
fix_options.complexity: LOW — add a config option like --peer-urls 'psychology-agent=http://localhost:8082,operations-agent=http://localhost:8083'.
fix_options.drawback: Only works when agents co-locate on the same host. Does not solve the public dashboard access problem.

fix_options.title: RECOMMENDED — FIX-1 + FIX-3 combined
fix_options.description: Port CollectRemoteStates to the meshd repo (FIX-1) so each agent's status includes peer data via git show. Configure the pulse aggregator to use localhost URLs (FIX-3) so the interagent dashboard can aggregate. This requires no external infrastructure and works with the current co-located setup.
fix_options.complexity: LOW — both changes are straightforward. FIX-1 copies existing Go code. FIX-3 adds a config field.
fix_options.drawback: Still no public dashboard access. FIX-2 remains the path for that.

additional_observation.observatory_schema_mismatch: observatory-agent mesh-state uses 'trust_budget' instead of 'autonomy_budget' as the key name. The dashboard template (mesh.html line 151) looks for 'autonomy_budget'. Even after fixing RC-1/RC-2, observatory's budget column will show '—' unless the template handles both key names or observatory normalizes to 'autonomy_budget'.

epistemic_flags: Investigation performed from psychology-agent's host. Cannot verify whether Chromabook's meshd instances behave identically (different config, different state.db).

The meshd repo (/Users/kashif/Projects/meshd/) may have diverged from the platform/ Go source in ways beyond CollectRemoteStates. A full diff between the two codebases would reveal the complete divergence scope.

action_gate.gate_condition: Ops to review fix options and implement. FIX-4 (recommended) requires changes to the meshd repo, not the psychology-agent repo. Psychology-agent's platform/ Go source already has the correct CollectRemoteStates code to port.
